title: Channels
description: How goroutines talk to each other.

slides.title: Passing Messages ✉️
slides.content:
## Connecting Goroutines

If you have multiple goroutines running in the background, they often need to share data or tell each other when they are finished.

In Go, they do this by sending data through **Channels**.
Think of a channel like a literal pipe that connects two goroutines.

slides.title: Creating and Using a Channel 🛠️
slides.content: You create a channel using `make(chan Type)`. You send data *into* a channel using `<-`.

quizData.question: Which operator is used to send data *into* a channel named `myChan`?

slides.title: Channels Block 🛑
slides.content:
## Built-in Synchronization

When a goroutine sends data into a channel (`ch <- data`), it **stops and waits** until another goroutine is ready to pull that data out (`<-ch`).

This means channels don't just send data; they automatically synchronize your running tasks so they don't trip over each other!
